model_narrative: We are mapping the shortest sea routes for a world phosphate network. From each source port we send flow out across a network of directed sea links, where every link carries a known distance in nautical miles. For each source we choose how much flow to push along each directed link. The goal is to make the total routed length, summed over every source and every link, as small as possible.

sets.name: nodes
sets.doc: the full set of ports / nodes in the sea-route network

sets.name: src
sets.doc: the source nodes; the origins that flow is routed out from

sets.name: dest
sets.doc: the destination nodes that must be reached from the sources

sets.name: arcs
sets.doc: the directed arcs that carry a distance; each physical link is stored both ways, so (n, np) and (np, n) are separate members

params.name: darc
params.doc: the distance of each directed arc, in nautical miles

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of flow sent from a given source along a given directed arc

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total routed length over all sources and arcs, in nautical miles

description: For each source and for every node that is not that source itself, the total flow from that source arriving into the node must be at least one unit greater than the total flow from that source leaving the node. A source node imposes no such requirement on itself.
expected_pyomo:
def nb_rule(model, s, n):
    if n == s:
        return Constraint.Skip
    inflow = sum(model.x[s, a, b] for (a, b) in model.arcs if b == n)
    outflow = sum(model.x[s, a, b] for (a, b) in model.arcs if a == n)
    return inflow >= outflow + 1
model.nb = Constraint(model.src, model.nodes, rule=nb_rule)

For each source we choose how much flow to push along each directed link. The goal is to make the total routed length, summed over every source and every link, as small as possible.

description: The total routed length accounts for every bit of flow on every link. Across all sources and all directed links, take the distance of each link times the flow that source sends along it, and set the total length equal to the sum of all those amounts.

model_narrative: We are mapping the shortest sea routes for a world phosphate network. From each source port we send flow out across a network of directed sea links, where every link carries a known distance in nautical miles. For each source we choose how much flow to push along each directed link. The goal is to make the total routed length, summed over every source and every link, as small as possible.

sets.name: nodes
sets.doc: the full set of ports / nodes in the sea-route network

sets.name: src
sets.doc: the source nodes; the origins that flow is routed out from

sets.name: dest
sets.doc: the destination nodes that must be reached from the sources

sets.name: arcs
sets.doc: the directed arcs that carry a distance; each physical link is stored both ways, so (n, np) and (np, n) are separate members

params.name: darc
params.doc: the distance of each directed arc, in nautical miles

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of flow sent from a given source along a given directed arc

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total routed length over all sources and arcs, in nautical miles

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, at every node other than a source itself, require that the flow arriving into that node from a given source exceeds the flow leaving that node by at least one unit, so that each node is reached from each source. Finally, define the total routed length by accumulating, over every source and every directed link, the distance of that link multiplied by the flow the source sends along it, and set the total length equal to that accumulated amount.
